title: scratch not recognized in a multi-stage build
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
When using scratch in  a multi-stage build the layer does not build. This worked in version 1.23.x but no longer works in 1.27 and 1.28. I haven't tried on versions between 1.23 and 1.27
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
export BUILDAH_LAYERS=true
export STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs
```

My Dockerfile
```
FROM alpine AS build
RUN echo "test" > file1
RUN echo "test2" > file2

FROM scratch AS test
COPY --from=build file1 /

FROM alpine
COPY --from=build file2 /
```
 `buildah build .` (Try on 1.23.x and 1.27.x or 1.28.0 with different results)

**Describe the results you received:**
`FROM scratch AS test` was executed only in 1.23.1 but did not execute on 1.27+

**Describe the results you expected:**
`FROM scratch AS test` should always be executed

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**
Tried with 2 versions.

```
buildah-1.23.1-1.fc35.x86_64 (works)
buildah-1.27.0-1.fc36.x86_64 (does not work)
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.27.0
Go Version:      go1.18.4
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.2
image Version:   5.22.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Tue Aug  9 02:26:49 2022
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

AND

```
Version:         1.23.1
Go Version:      go1.16.8
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.8.1
image Version:   5.16.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Tue Sep 28 18:26:37 2021
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```


**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]

[storage.options]

[storage.options.overlay]

[storage.options.thinpool]

On 1.23.x
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
"/var/lib/shared",
]

[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
mountopt = "nodev,fsync=0"

[storage.options.thinpool]
```

comments.body:
Hi @flyingsaucerer ,

The behavior is correct, reason the stage `FROM scratch AS test` is not executed is because the stage is skipped since it is not used in the target stage, buildah marks unused stages as `to-be-skipped` and does not executed them in order to prevent compute.

Either use `FROM scratch AS TEST` in target stage or set `--skip-unused-stages=false` as CLI flag

Containerfile which uses the ignored stage
```
FROM alpine AS build
RUN echo "test" > file1
RUN echo "test2" > file2

FROM scratch AS test
COPY --from=build file1 /

FROM alpine
COPY --from=test file1 /
COPY --from=build file2 /
```

See PR: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4004 

I'm closing this issue but please feel free to comment below or reopen if i missed something
Thanks
